frames: The two-segment pendulum stands perfectly upright, with its base segment firmly on the ground.
The pendulum begins to tilt slowly to the right, maintaining its base contact.
The tilt increases, and the upper segment starts to descend at a noticeable pace.
The pendulum is now significantly angled, smoothly falling towards the horizontal plane.
The pendulum continues its descent, with the top segment approaching the ground rapidly.
The pendulum is almost entirely horizontal, with its upper segment nearly parallel to the surface.
The pendulum makes full contact with the ground, lying flat and appearing to settle.
The pendulum is completely fallen and remains motionless on the checkered floor.
